question: Will Perplexity not IPO by December 31, 2027?
description:
This market will resolve based on Perplexity's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no Perplexity IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Perplexity’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no Perplexity IPO before 2028 at 50.5% implied probability, driven by the AI search startup's lack of public filing plans and CEO Aravind Srinivas's emphasis on sustained product innovation over rushed liquidity events amid volatile tech markets. Recent funding talks for a $500M+ round valuing the company at around $9 billion—up from $3 billion in April 2024—underscore aggressive private growth via tools like Perplexity Enterprise Pro and device integrations with Samsung, yet signal prolonged private status similar to OpenAI and Anthropic. The 21.8% odds for a $40–50 billion debut reflect optimism on explosive revenue scaling toward $100 million ARR, but regulatory scrutiny on AI data practices and broader IPO hesitancy temper higher brackets.

question: Will Trump endorse María Corina Machado for Venezuela president in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump publicly endorses or suggests María Corina Machado as Venezuelan state leader or president by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Donald Trump announces that he would prefer or endorses another individual, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Donald Trump or one of his representatives, or a consensus of credible reporting of Trump's endorsement.

events.title: Will Trump endorse María Corina Machado for Venezuela president in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump publicly endorses or suggests María Corina Machado as Venezuelan state leader or president by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Donald Trump announces that he would prefer or endorses another individual, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Donald Trump or one of his representatives, or a consensus of credible reporting of Trump's endorsement.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 82.5% implied probability against Donald Trump endorsing Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado for the 2026 presidential race, driven primarily by the absence of any official statement or action from Trump despite their April 2024 Mar-a-Lago meeting where he praised her leadership. Machado remains barred from office by Venezuelan authorities until 2036, complicating her candidacy prospects amid ongoing election disputes following the disputed July 2024 vote. Recent Trump remarks on Venezuela have focused on sanctions, oil production, and Maduro regime criticism without naming Machado as a preferred successor, amid his U.S. campaign priorities. Upcoming U.S. election outcomes could influence future foreign policy signals, but current pricing underscores skepticism of a specific endorsement.

question: Will the Bank of Israel decrease the Bank of Israel Interest Rate after the March decision?
description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the Bank of Israel Interest Rate resulting from the Bank of Israel’s March monetary policy decision, relative to the level it was prior to this decision.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Israel after its March 30, 2026 monetary policy decision, as listed on the official Bank of Israel interest rate decision schedule: https://www.boi.org.il/en/economic-roles/monetary-policy/interest-rate-announcement-dates-2025-2026/#

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Israel's announcement of their March 30, 2026 decision with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the Bank of Israel Interest Rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: Bank of Israel Decision in March?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the Bank of Israel Interest Rate resulting from the Bank of Israel’s March monetary policy decision, relative to the level it was prior to this decision.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Israel after its March 30, 2026 monetary policy decision, as listed on the official Bank of Israel interest rate decision schedule: https://www.boi.org.il/en/economic-roles/monetary-policy/interest-rate-announcement-dates-2025-2026/#

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Israel's announcement of their March 30, 2026 decision with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the Bank of Israel Interest Rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bank of Israel traders overwhelmingly favor no change in the March 24 interest rate decision at 93%, reflecting stable inflation near the 1-3% target band, with January CPI at 3.1% year-over-year amid resilient GDP growth despite ongoing Gaza and Lebanon conflicts. Recent minutes from the February meeting highlighted a data-dependent pause at 4.5%, supported by moderating wage pressures and steady shekel amid U.S. rate cut expectations. This commanding consensus draws from historical precedent of prolonged holds during uncertainty. Realistic challenges include a surprise inflation spike from supply disruptions or weakening labor data prompting a cut, though upside risks for hikes remain minimal absent renewed price pressures.

question: Will the U.S. invade Colombia in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Colombian land territory by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Colombia or the United States, as of market creation, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will the U.S. invade Colombia in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Colombian land territory by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Colombia or the United States, as of market creation, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Colombia remains a key U.S. ally in counter-narcotics efforts and regional stability, receiving over $500 million in annual U.S. aid with no indications of military confrontation. Recent diplomatic engagements, including joint operations against drug cartels and cooperation on Venezuelan migration, underscore partnership rather than hostility. President-elect Trump's rhetoric on Latin American cartels has focused on sanctions, deportations, and economic pressure—not invasion—while official Pentagon statements prioritize threats in the Middle East and Indo-Pacific. Absent any escalatory events like border incursions or alliance ruptures, traders price a 93% "No" probability, aligning with historical U.S. restraint in Latin America since the 1989 Panama intervention and the improbability of a new ground war amid domestic fiscal constraints.

question: Israel x Syria security agreement by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official security agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed deal between the governments of Israel and Syria by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market refers only to agreements which directly address border security and demarcation, normalization, or diplomatic recognition or otherwise creates a formalized security framework between the two states.    

Ceasefire announcements or other announced de-escalations such as those relating to the July 16-18 skirmishes with the Druze, or any other future military engagements, which do not address the broader security relationship will not qualify.

Only deals which are officially announced will qualify. Informal announcements which do not constitute a formalized agreement will not count.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the Israeli and Syrian governments. However, an agreement which is announced by only of the parties will qualify if an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirms that such a formal agreement has been reached.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on an Israel-Syria security agreement remains subdued, with low implied probabilities reflecting persistent military tensions following Bashar al-Assad's ouster in December 2024. Israel's ground incursion into the Golan Heights buffer zone and airstrikes on Syrian military sites underscore security concerns over potential Hezbollah or Iranian threats, despite interim Syrian leader Ahmed al-Sharaa's overtures for normalized ties and demilitarization pledges. No formal negotiations have materialized, prioritizing Israeli demands for verifiable guarantees. Upcoming U.S. policy shifts post-January 20 inauguration and potential Damascus-Tel Aviv contacts could catalyze movement, though historical mistrust and regional volatility sustain trader caution.

question: Will Darren Fletcher be appointed as manager of Manchester United?
description:
This market will resolve according to the person who is appointed as the next permanent manager of Manchester United.

If no permanent manager is appointed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other." 

Appointments of 'interim,' 'caretaker,' or other non-permanent managers will not impact this market's resolution.

An announcement of a new permanent manager's appointment before this market's close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option, regardless of when the announced appointment goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Manchester United; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Next Manchester United manager?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the person who is appointed as the next permanent manager of Manchester United.

If no permanent manager is appointed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other." 

Appointments of 'interim,' 'caretaker,' or other non-permanent managers will not impact this market's resolution.

An announcement of a new permanent manager's appointment before this market's close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option, regardless of when the announced appointment goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Manchester United; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Carrick leads trader consensus at 56.5% implied probability as next Manchester United manager following Erik ten Hag's dismissal on October 28 after just three Premier League wins in 14 games, with Ruud van Nistelrooy installed as interim but suffering a 2-1 loss to West Ham. Carrick's prior successful caretaker spell in 2021—four games unbeaten—plus his club legend status and Middlesbrough promotion push make him the pragmatic internal frontrunner amid stalled external pursuits like Ruben Amorim's release clause hurdles at Sporting. Oliver Glasner's Crystal Palace surge (unbeaten in seven) elevates him to 12.5%, while availability boosts Luis Enrique (10.8%) and Thomas Tuchel (7.2%), reflecting wisdom of crowds on short-term stability over high-profile risks.

question: Viktor Orbán out by December 31, 2026?

An announcement of Viktor Orbán's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 63.5% implied probability that Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán will no longer hold office by December 31, 2026, driven primarily by the rapid rise of opposition challenger Péter Magyar's Tisza party, which recent polls show narrowing Fidesz's lead to within striking distance for the 2026 parliamentary election. Massive protests in Budapest on October 14, 2024— the largest in over a decade—opposed the new Sovereignty Protection Office law, amplifying public discontent over perceived authoritarian measures, economic stagnation, and frozen EU funds. While Fidesz retains advantages in media and institutions, trader sentiment highlights shifting momentum from EU parliamentary losses and scandals, though outcomes remain uncertain amid Hungary's fragmented opposition history.

question: Will the U.S. invade Mexico in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Mexican land territory by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Mexico or the United States, as of market creation, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus assigns a 91.5% implied probability to no U.S. invasion of Mexico in 2026, driven by the complete absence of official U.S. policy proposals, military planning, or congressional authorization for such action. U.S.-Mexico relations remain anchored in the USMCA trade framework and joint operations against cartels, as affirmed in recent diplomatic talks between President Biden and President Sheinbaum, who has firmly rejected foreign military intervention. While campaign rhetoric from President-elect Trump emphasizes designating cartels as terrorists and potential targeted strikes with Mexican cooperation, it explicitly avoids invasion scenarios. International law, alliance commitments, and historical non-aggression since the 19th century reinforce this low-risk assessment amid ongoing bilateral security collaboration.

question: Forsen to get signed by an LCK prog org by Jun 30?
description:
The market resolves to "Yes" if Sebastian "Forsen" Fors is officially announced as signed or recruited by an LCK organization by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A professional League of Legends LCK organization is any of the 10 franchised organizations that field a paid League of Legends roster in the LCK.

“Signs” refers to Forsen entering into a paid contract with an organization to compete in League of Legends for that organization. Trials or other relationships that do not include a signed professional playing contract will not count. Sponsorship agreements or other contracts that do not pay Forsen to compete in League of Legends for a specific organization will also not count.

A signed professional contract between Forsen and a qualifying LCK organization within this market’s timeframe will qualify even if its effective start date is after June 30, 2026.

The primary resolution source will be official announcements from the team involved and Forsen, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probabilities for invocation of the Insurrection Act by President Biden before January 20 or by incoming President Trump shortly after inauguration, driven by the law's rarity—last used in 1992 for Los Angeles riots—and legal hurdles requiring civil disorder threats unmet by current conditions. Recent catalysts include Trump's campaign pledges for mass deportations potentially testing military deployment limits, amid muted campus protests and no widespread insurrection signals. Historical precedents like 2020 unrest show threats rarely materialize without escalation. Key upcoming events: January 20 inauguration amid possible demonstrations, early executive orders on borders, and congressional oversight, any sparking disorder could shift odds higher.

question: Will Drake release Iceman before GTA VI?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Drake officially releases Iceman before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Iceman refers to the upcoming studio album by Drake of that title, which has been promoted through videos released with the titles “Iceman Episode (1-4)”, and the singles “What Did I Miss”, “Which One”, and “Dog House.”

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

The full Iceman studio album must be released in order to resolve this market. Further singles or Iceman video episodes will not be sufficient to resolve this market. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify. A consensus of credible reporting that a released album is the Iceman project may be used if Drake releases the album under a different name.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's "What will happen before GTA VI?" market tilts toward high-probability outcomes like major trailer drops or rival game launches, driven by Rockstar's reaffirmed Fall 2025 console release during Take-Two's May earnings call, implying a narrow window for preceding events. question: New "Stranger Things" episode released by December 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Netflix officially releases a new episode of Stranger Things (i.e., an episode that was not previously available to stream on Netflix) between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, an “episode” must be listed as a distinct episode of Stranger Things on Netflix and be playable for general subscribers in the United States. A behind-the-scenes featurette, documentary, trailer, recap, cast interview, deleted scenes compilation, or other bonus content will not count unless it is clearly presented by Netflix as an official numbered or titled episode of the series.

If Netflix releases an alternate cut, extended cut, or “secret” version of an already-released episode, it will not count unless it is listed as a separate episode entry on Netflix.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Stranger Things title page on Netflix (episode list and availability), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Israel strike on Yemen by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Yemeni soil or any official Yemen embassy or consulate by the listed date, 11:59 PM Israeli local time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact Yemen ground territory or any official Yemen embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Yemen soil is hit by an Israeli missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on Yemen territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Traders eye upcoming Houthi salvos or Netanyahu announcements as key catalysts before year-end.

question: Will US annex any territory in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States officially annexes any territory by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Annexation is defined as an official declaration or legal act by the US government claiming sovereignty over territory they were not claiming at the time of this market's creation.

Qualifying examples of annexation include the Louisiana Purchase, however instances where settlers claim administrative control over land without a formal annexation will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will Hong Wang win the 2026 Fields Medal?
description:
The Fields Medal is a prize regarded as the top award in the field of mathematics worldwide. It is awarded to two, three, or four mathematicians under 40 years of age at the International Congress of the International Mathematical Union (IMU), a meeting that takes place every four years. The International Congress of Mathematicians 2026 (ICM 2026) is scheduled to take place from July 23 to July 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the winners of the 2026 Fields medal.

If the 2026 Fields medalists are not announced by August 15, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the IMU (https://www.mathunion.org/imu-awards/fields-medal), however other credible reporting may be used.

It is awarded to two, three, or four mathematicians under 40 years of age at the International Congress of the International Mathematical Union (IMU), a meeting that takes place every four years. The International Congress of Mathematicians 2026 (ICM 2026) is scheduled to take place from July 23 to July 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the winners of the 2026 Fields medal.

If the 2026 Fields medalists are not announced by August 15, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the IMU (https://www.mathunion.org/imu-awards/fields-medal), however other credible reporting may be used.

question: Any EU nation's debt downgraded before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the long-term sovereign credit letter rating of any European Union member country is downgraded by any of the three major credit rating agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) at any point between the date of market creation and December 31, 2026 11:59pm ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Any EU nation's debt downgraded before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the long-term sovereign credit letter rating of any European Union member country is downgraded by any of the three major credit rating agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) at any point between the date of market creation and December 31, 2026 11:59pm ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Moody's November 18 review for possible downgrade of France's Aa3 sovereign rating amid fiscal deterioration and political gridlock has fueled trader consensus for a 69% implied probability of any EU nation facing a debt downgrade before 2027. France's debt-to-GDP ratio exceeds 110%, compounded by budget shortfalls and a fragile minority government post-snap elections, while Italy's 140% ratio and Greece's ongoing vulnerabilities add pressure. Rating agencies like S&P and Fitch have issued negative outlooks for several eurozone peers, including Belgium and Romania, amid rising defense and green spending demands. Traders price in elevated risks from persistent deficits and ECB tightening limits, with scheduled agency reviews likely to catalyze shifts.

question: KHL: Shanghai Dragons vs. HC Sochi
description:
In the upcoming KHL game, scheduled for 2026-01-12:
If Shanghai Dragons win, the market will resolve to "Shanghai Dragons".
If HC Sochi win, the market will resolve to "HC Sochi".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

No major injuries reported on either side per official updates, but Sochi's edge in head-to-head history (3-1 since 2022) and home-ice advantage at Adler Arena amplify trader consensus on their dominance. Shanghai's road woes—1-6 away—further tilt the ice toward the favorites amid the Eastern Conference grind.

question: Will Carlos Alcaraz win a calendar Grand Slam in 2026?
description:
This is a market to predict who will win a men's tennis Calendar Grand Slam in 2026.

This market resolves to the single male player who wins the Men’s Singles titles at all four Grand Slam tournaments in 2026. The relevant 2026 Grand Slams are the Men’s Singles tournaments in the 2026 Australian Open, French Open, U.S. Open, and Wimbledon.

For the purpose of this market, a tournament victory is valid regardless of whether the final was won via a walkover, a mid-match retirement, or a standard completed match.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to achieve a Calendar Grand Slam in 2026 (e.g. Alcaraz does not win the 2026 Australian Open) this market will resolve immediately to "No". 

If at any point it becomes impossible for anyone to achieve a Calendar Grand Slam in 2026 this market will resolve immediately to "None".

Otherwise, this market will resolve when the results of the final 2026 Grand Slam are official.

If any of the 2026 Grand Slam Men’s Singles Tournaments are cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “None”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the organizers of the Grand Slam tournaments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Who will win a Calendar Grand Slam in 2026?
events.description:
This is a market to predict who will win a men's tennis Calendar Grand Slam in 2026.

This market resolves to the single male player who wins the Men’s Singles titles at all four Grand Slam tournaments in 2026. The relevant 2026 Grand Slams are the Men’s Singles tournaments in the 2026 Australian Open, French Open, U.S. Open, and Wimbledon.

For the purpose of this market, a tournament victory is valid regardless of whether the final was won via a walkover, a mid-match retirement, or a standard completed match.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to achieve a Calendar Grand Slam in 2026 (e.g. Alcaraz does not win the 2026 Australian Open) this market will resolve immediately to "No". 

If at any point it becomes impossible for any listed player to achieve a Calendar Grand Slam in 2026 (e.g. Alcaraz wins the 2026 Australian Open but loses in the first round of 2026 French Open) this market will resolve immediately to "None". 

Otherwise, this market will resolve when the results of the final 2026 Grand Slam are official.

If any of the 2026 Grand Slam Men’s Singles Tournaments are cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “None”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the organizers of the Grand Slam tournaments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The extreme rarity of a Calendar Grand Slam—unachieved by any man since Rod Laver in 1969—anchors trader consensus at 89.5% for "None" in 2026, amplified by modern tennis's depth, surface diversity, injury risks, and grueling schedule that have prevented dominance across Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, and US Open. Carlos Alcaraz's 10.8% share stems from his youth (turning 23), three-slam versatility (2022 US Open, multiple French and Wimbledon titles), and recent 2024 clay-grass mastery, yet his Australian Open drought and past injuries temper expectations. Realistic challenges require Alcaraz sustaining peak health through a full season amid rising threats from Jannik Sinner and others, an improbable feat given historical precedents.

question: Deel IPO by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Deel completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements or credible news sources. 

The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If Deel merges with another entity, is acquired, or ceases to exist before the market resolves, the market will also resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Deel IPO by March 31?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects near-certainty at 99.6% odds against a Deel IPO by March 31, 2025, driven primarily by the complete absence of official announcements, SEC filings, or banker engagements signaling preparations—the standard prelude to any public listing. Deel's leadership has emphasized global payroll expansion and AI-driven compliance tools over monetizing via markets, amid a fintech sector wary of volatile IPO windows post-2022 downturns. Historical precedents show even streamlined processes take 4-6 months from S-1 filing to debut. Realistic upset scenarios include a surprise confidential draft registration with accelerated SEC review, unforeseen regulatory fast-tracks for cross-border ops, or blockbuster Q4 earnings prompting a rush, though each faces steep logistical and market hurdles.

question: Will Iván Arias win the 2026 La Paz mayoral election?
description:
The La Paz mayoral election is scheduled to take place in Bolivia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Bolivian electoral authority, the Tribunal Supremo Electoral (TSE) (https://www.oep.org.bo).

eventMetadata.context_description: César Dockweiler's dominant 58% implied probability in the La Paz mayoral race reflects trader consensus on his polling lead, driven by Comunidad Ciudadana's opposition momentum against the ruling MAS amid Bolivia's polarized national politics. Recent surveys from Ciesmori and others show Dockweiler at 40-50%, boosted by strong youth and middle-class turnout projections and endorsements from anti-Arce figures. Jhonny Plata's 10% odds capture MAS loyalists despite party infighting and lower urban appeal. Trailing candidates like Miguel Roca and Iván Arias hold slim chances tied to niche coalitions. Markets eye final debates and voter registration data ahead of the March 2025 vote, where turnout could sway outcomes.

description:
The La Paz mayoral election is scheduled to take place in Bolivia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Bolivian electoral authority, the Tribunal Supremo Electoral (TSE) (https://www.oep.org.bo).

events.title: La Paz Mayoral Election Winner (Bolivia)
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: César Dockweiler's dominant 58% implied probability in the La Paz mayoral race reflects trader consensus on his polling lead, driven by Comunidad Ciudadana's opposition momentum against the ruling MAS amid Bolivia's polarized national politics. Recent surveys from Ciesmori and others show Dockweiler at 40-50%, boosted by strong youth and middle-class turnout projections and endorsements from anti-Arce figures. Jhonny Plata's 10% odds capture MAS loyalists despite party infighting and lower urban appeal. Trailing candidates like Miguel Roca and Iván Arias hold slim chances tied to niche coalitions. Markets eye final debates and voter registration data ahead of the March 2025 vote, where turnout could sway outcomes.

question: Space FDV above $40M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Space's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Space (https://x.com/intodotspace) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Jhonny Fernández win the 2026 Santa Cruz de la Sierra mayoral election?
description:
The Santa Cruz de la Sierra mayoral election is scheduled to take place in Bolivia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Bolivian electoral authority, the Tribunal Supremo Electoral (TSE) (https://www.oep.org.bo).

events.title: Santa Cruz de la Sierra Mayoral Election Winner (Bolivia)
events.description:
The Santa Cruz de la Sierra mayoral election is scheduled to take place in Bolivia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Bolivian electoral authority, the Tribunal Supremo Electoral (TSE) (https://www.oep.org.bo).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Manuel Saavedra as the Santa Cruz de la Sierra mayoral election winner, with odds implying a 97% probability after Bolivia's electoral tribunal released TREP quick count results showing him leading by over 30 points. Saavedra's strong performance stems from robust support in this opposition-leaning department, capitalizing on local dissatisfaction with the ruling MAS party's candidate Angélica Sosa amid economic pressures and regional autonomy debates. Recent developments, including validated exit polls aligning with preliminary tallies from March 2021 subnational voting, have solidified his position. Realistic challenges include formal computation disputes or recounts if rural precincts shift margins, though current evidence points to a decisive outcome absent major irregularities.

question: Will SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12 launch by March 31?
description:
If the 12th SpaceX Starship successfully launches from its launch pad by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Yes”. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The outcome of any launch will be corroborated by examining official video provided by SpaceX (https://www.youtube.com/c/SpaceX), as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

Any subsequent anomaly (e.g., an explosion) after the launch will have no bearing on the outcome. Any name change of the Starship with another vehicle will have no bearing on the outcome of the market.

events.title: SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12
events.description: This is a market on the outcome of the SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Starship Flight 5's historic booster catch on October 13 has surged trader optimism for Flight Test 12, with Polymarket odds implying over 60% probability of a full-duration orbital flight success by mid-2026, reflecting SpaceX's rapid iteration pace. Elon Musk's announcements of Raptor 3 engine upgrades and ship 30/Booster 12 preparations underscore hardware maturity, while FAA licensing delays remain the key risk after Flight 5 mishap probes cleared quickly. Competitive pressures from Blue Origin's New Glenn are muted, as Starship leads in payload capacity and reusability; traders eye Flight 6 (targeted November) as a pivotal test of back-to-back catches, potentially catalyzing odds shifts ahead of 2025's multi-flight cadence.

events.title: Sucre Mayoral Election Winner (Bolivia)
events.description:
The Sucre mayoral election is scheduled to take place in Bolivia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Enrique Leaño at 48.7% and Cristian Sanabria at 46.5% for Sucre's mayoral contest in Bolivia, reflecting persistent polling tightness amid Chuquisaca's polarized electorate between MAS-aligned and opposition forces. Recent developments, including Leaño's endorsement from national MAS leaders and Sanabria's community mobilization efforts, have neutralized momentum, keeping the race within margin-of-error bounds per local surveys from late 2024. Lower-tier candidates like Pablo Arízaga dilute votes without breaking the deadlock. Separation could emerge from final preelection polls, voter turnout in urban vs. rural precincts, or last-minute scandals ahead of the March 2025 vote, underscoring Bolivia's competitive subnational dynamics.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Enrique Leaño at 48.7% and Cristian Sanabria at 46.5% for Sucre's mayoral contest in Bolivia, reflecting persistent polling tightness amid Chuquisaca's polarized electorate between MAS-aligned and opposition forces. Lower-tier candidates like Pablo Arízaga dilute votes without breaking the deadlock. Separation could emerge from final preelection polls, voter turnout in urban vs. rural precincts, or last-minute scandals ahead of the March 2025 vote, underscoring Bolivia's competitive subnational dynamics.

question: Will the UK’s 2026 inflation be less than 1.0%?
description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in the U.K. over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the Office for National Statistics (ONS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026, according to the monthly ONS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the ONS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026 (https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/previousreleases), currently scheduled to be released on January 20, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by choosing the report for the relevant month from the previous releases page for price indices on the ONS website (https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/previousreleases). The relevant figure can be found in the "Consumer price inflation rates" table under the column labeled "CPI 12-month rate (%)" for the relevant month.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly ONS CPI news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://www.ons.gov.uk/releasecalendar

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a fragmented outlook for UK annual inflation in 2026, with 4.5%+ leading at 29% implied probability amid sticky services inflation near 5% and robust wage growth exceeding 5%, closely rivaling 1.5-2.4% bands totaling 74% combined but split across buckets. Primary drivers include the Bank of England's cautious rate cuts—now at 4.5%—against fiscal expansion from the Autumn Budget boosting demand pressures, while global energy volatility adds upside risk. Key differentiators hinge on upcoming CPI releases through mid-2025 and BoE's February Inflation Report; persistent core pressures above 3% could solidify high-end odds, but recessionary GDP drags might validate sub-2% trader bets.

This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in the U.K. over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the Office for National Statistics (ONS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026, according to the monthly ONS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the ONS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026 (https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/previousreleases), currently scheduled to be released on January 20, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by choosing the report for the relevant month from the previous releases page for price indices on the ONS website (https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/previousreleases). The relevant figure can be found in the "Consumer price inflation rates" table under the column labeled "CPI 12-month rate (%)" for the relevant month.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly ONS CPI news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://www.ons.gov.uk/releasecalendar

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a fragmented outlook for UK annual inflation in 2026, with 4.5%+ leading at 29% implied probability amid sticky services inflation near 5% and robust wage growth exceeding 5%, closely rivaling 1.5-2.4% bands totaling 74% combined but split across buckets. Primary drivers include the Bank of England's cautious rate cuts—now at 4.5%—against fiscal expansion from the Autumn Budget boosting demand pressures, while global energy volatility adds upside risk. Key differentiators hinge on upcoming CPI releases through mid-2025 and BoE's February Inflation Report; persistent core pressures above 3% could solidify high-end odds, but recessionary GDP drags might validate sub-2% trader bets.

question: Will the Christian Democratic Party (PDC) win the most gubernatorial elections in Bolivia in 2026?
description:
Bolivia’s gubernatorial elections are scheduled to be held on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the party whose official candidates win the most gubernatorial elections for Bolivia’s departments during these elections.

A candidate will be considered an official candidate of a party if they are officially nominated by that party and are registered for the relevant election in affiliation with that party. Independent candidates will not count for any party.

Bolivia’s departments include the following:
La Paz, Santa Cruz, Cochabamba, Oruro, Potosí, Tarija, Beni, Pando, and Chuquisaca.

A party will have won as soon as it becomes mathematically impossible for any other party to equal or surpass its number of wins in these elections.

In the case of a tie between two or more parties for the greatest number of gubernatorial elections won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed name (not abbreviation) comes first in alphabetical order.

This market includes any potential second round. Resolution of this market will be based on the results of the relevant elections, once those results are official. This market will remain open until a party has won or until the results of all of the relevant elections are made official. If the results of any of the relevant 2026 Bolivian gubernatorial elections aren’t known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the winning party will be determined based on the available results up to that point. If none of the results of the relevant 2026 Bolivian gubernatorial elections are known by that time, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Bolivian electoral authority, the Tribunal Supremo Electoral (TSE) (https://www.oep.org.bo).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions LIBRE (46%) as the frontrunner to win the most governorships in Bolivia's 2026 subnational elections, driven by ruling MAS-IPSP's internal fracture between President Luis Arce's faction and Evo Morales supporters, slashing MAS odds to 8.9%. Recent Senate expulsion of Morales allies in September 2024 and his vow for parallel candidacies have accelerated opposition momentum, elevating Santa Cruz-based autonomy party APB Súmate (35.5%), alongside AP (32.5%), PDC (31.5%), and UNIDAD (28.5%). Regional polls reflect MAS erosion in strongholds like Cochabamba amid economic woes and protests, though vote fragmentation risks persist ahead of candidate filings.

question: Venezuela coup attempt by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a widely reported coup attempt in Venezuela at any point between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

A coup attempt is defined as a coordinated and deliberate effort by military, security forces, or other state actors (or factions thereof) to overthrow or unlawfully seize control of the Venezuelan government or its leadership. Revolutionary actions by non-state forces, isolated protests, or general unrest will not alone qualify.

Claims by the Venezuelan government that a coup attempt was foiled—such as through arrests or disruption of a planned operation—will only count toward a "Yes" resolution if credible independent sources widely report and explicitly characterize the event as a coup attempt. Foiled plots or arrests that occur without any attempted execution of the coup will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Venezuela coup attempt by March 31?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects near-certainty at 98.8% that no coup attempt will occur in Venezuela by March 31, driven primarily by President Nicolás Maduro's longstanding control over the military and security forces, with no credible reports of defections or plotting from primary sources. Recent developments, including the regime's effective suppression of post-2024 election protests and opposition focus on diplomatic pressure rather than armed action, reinforce this stability amid economic sanctions and oil revenue flows. Historical failures of past coup efforts, like those in 2019-2020, further bolster confidence in the status quo. Realistic shifts could stem from unforeseen military mutinies or sharp external escalations, though evidence remains absent.

Claims by the Iranian government that a coup attempt was foiled—such as through arrests or disruption of a planned operation—will only count toward a "Yes" resolution if credible independent sources widely report and explicitly characterize the event as a coup attempt. Foiled plots or arrests that occur without any attempted execution of the coup will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors no coup attempt in Iran by June 30, with "No" shares at 73.5%, driven by the absence of credible evidence for organized internal challenges to the regime. Recent Israeli airstrikes on June 13 killed senior IRGC commanders, prompting Iranian missile retaliation, yet official statements from Supreme Leader Khamenei and military leaders affirm unified control and stability, with no reports of defections, protests, or power vacuums. Historical resilience during past crises, including protests and sanctions, supports skepticism of rapid overthrow. Markets reflect this low-risk assessment absent new catalysts like mass unrest or elite fractures.

question: Amouranth divorced by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Kaitlyn Siragusa (known as Amouranth) is legally divorced from her husband, Nick Lee, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A final divorce decree or equivalent court order issued by a competent jurisdiction by the specified date will be required for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of any prior announcements of intention to divorce or separations.

The primary resolution source will be public court records from the relevant jurisdiction. A consensus of credible media reporting confirming the existence of such a decree may also be used.

Personal statements, social media posts, or announcements by Amouranth, Nick Lee, or their representatives will not be sufficient on their own for resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' strong 90% consensus on "No" hinges on the lengthy timeline of Amouranth's ongoing divorce proceedings, mirroring a star player sidelined by a long-term injury with no quick return before the June 30 deadline. Filed in mid-March 2024 under Texas no-fault rules requiring a 60-day waiting period—already passed without finalization—recent form shows no accelerated court hearings or settlements announced by official filings or her updates. Matchup dynamics favor the status quo, as uncontested divorces often drag into months amid backlog, with her ex's response pending and no momentum shift from recent streams or posts. Historical precedents in high-profile splits underscore upsets are rare under tight deadlines, anchoring the wisdom of crowds.

question: Will Trump visit Greenland by March 31?
description:
If U.S. President Donald Trump visits the autonomous territory of Greenland by March 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of Greenland. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects near-certainty that President-elect Donald Trump will not visit Greenland by March 31, driven primarily by the absence of any official announcements, travel schedules, or diplomatic preparations from the Trump transition team or Danish authorities governing the territory. Trump's 2019 interest in acquiring Greenland sparked international backlash but yielded no visit then, and recent statements on Arctic strategy have not escalated to confirmed trips amid his packed post-inauguration agenda of cabinet confirmations and policy rollouts. With just weeks remaining, logistical hurdles and geopolitical sensitivities further solidify the "No" odds at 99.7%, though an unforeseen diplomatic summit or abrupt itinerary shift could theoretically alter probabilities.

question: Italy Judicial Reform Referendum passes?
description:
This market refers to a constitutional referendum expected to be held in Italy in the second half of March 2026 concerning proposed amendments to the Constitution of Italy related to the organization and functioning of the judiciary.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Italian judicial reform constitutional referendum (Nordio Reform Referendum), or a substantively equivalent referendum on the same constitutional amendments, is approved by a majority of valid votes cast in the nationwide referendum currently expected to be held in March 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

If the referendum is officially rescheduled, the same rule applies to the new ballot and its corresponding deadlines. This market will resolve to “No” if no qualifying referendum approving the judicial reform is passed by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source will be based on the official certified referendum results as published by the Italian Ministry of the Interior or an equivalent official Italian government authority. Subsequent litigation, administrative challenges, or failure of any post-approval implementation steps will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Italy's judicial reform referendum tilts slightly toward passage at 52.5% Yes odds, driven primarily by uncertainty over voter turnout exceeding the 50% quorum threshold required for validity. Recent polls from Ipsos and others indicate 30-40% support for the four reform questions—aimed at curbing magistrate privileges and separating judge-prosecutor careers—but predict turnout around 30-35%, favoring invalidation amid Prime Minister Meloni's government's abstention campaign. Competitive balance stems from potential poll underestimation of motivated opposition voters versus widespread apathy. Early voting data on June 8 evening or surprise mobilization could push Yes above 60%; confirmed low turnout would crash odds toward No.

question: Will the Eurozone's 2026 Annual Inflation be less than 1.0%?
description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in the Eurozone over the 12-month period ending December 2026 as reported by Eurostat.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) over the 12-month period ending December 2026, according to the monthly Eurostat report.

The resolution source for this market will be the Eurostat Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on January 19, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report on the Eurostat website (https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/en/) by selecting the "Database" option from the "Data" dropdown, opening the "Data navigation tree" folder, then the "Economy and finance" folder, then the "Prices (prc)" folder, then the "Harmonised index of consumer prices (HICP) (prc_hicp)" folder, and opening the file named "HICP - monthly data (annual rate of change) (prc_hicp_manr)". The relevant figure can be found in the column for the relevant month in the row marked "Euro area - 20 countries (from 2023)".

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Eurostat HICP news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/release-calendar or https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/statscal/ges/html/sthicp.en.html

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Eurozone annual inflation for 2026 reflects a tight contest between persistent upside risks and disinflationary forces, with market-implied odds favoring 3.1%+ at 42.1% and 2.8-3.0% at 37.1% over lower bands like 1.6-1.8% at 32.0%. Primary drivers include sticky services inflation and robust wage growth—Eurostat data shows November HICP at 2.3% YoY—bolstered by ECB's December staff projections of 1.9% for 2026, which traders view as optimistic amid geopolitical energy shocks and potential U.S. tariff spillovers. Differentiating factors hinge on ECB rate path efficacy versus fiscal loosening; upcoming March CPI and ECB March meeting will be pivotal, as probabilities imply trader consensus clusters above the 2% target.

You can find this report on the Eurostat website (https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/en/) by selecting the "Database" option from the "Data" dropdown, opening the "Data navigation tree" folder, then the "Economy and finance" folder, then the "Prices (prc)" folder, then the "Harmonised index of consumer prices (HICP) (prc_hicp)" folder, and opening the file named "HICP - monthly data (annual rate of change) (prc_hicp_manr)". The relevant figure can be found in the column for the relevant month in the row marked "Euro area - 20 countries (from 2023)".

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Eurostat HICP news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/release-calendar or https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/statscal/ges/html/sthicp.en.html

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Eurozone annual inflation for 2026 reflects a tight contest between persistent upside risks and disinflationary forces, with market-implied odds favoring 3.1%+ at 42.1% and 2.8-3.0% at 37.1% over lower bands like 1.6-1.8% at 32.0%. Primary drivers include sticky services inflation and robust wage growth—Eurostat data shows November HICP at 2.3% YoY—bolstered by ECB's December staff projections of 1.9% for 2026, which traders view as optimistic amid geopolitical energy shocks and potential U.S. tariff spillovers. Differentiating factors hinge on ECB rate path efficacy versus fiscal loosening; upcoming March CPI and ECB March meeting will be pivotal, as probabilities imply trader consensus clusters above the 2% target.

question: Will Valve remove Mirage from the Map Pool?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Valve removes the listed map from the official map pool by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and the removal lasts continuously for at least 48 hours.  Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Temporary or testing removals reversed within 48 hours do not count.

The "official map pool" refers to the Active Duty map group in CS2, used for competitive matchmaking (Premier mode) and professional tournaments.

For the purpose of this market, “Valve” refers to Valve Corporation, the developer and publisher of the Counter-Strike series.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Valve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Valve has made no official announcement on map removals from the CS2 active duty pool by June 30, anchoring trader consensus at low implied probabilities across options like Overpass, Ancient, or Mirage, reflecting the wisdom of crowds wary of unconfirmed speculation. Recent roster tweaks, including Overpass' benching in May amid pick/ban data showing low win rates (under 45% on some servers), fuel chatter, but Valve prioritizes majors over mid-cycle cuts. Upcoming factors include the July patch cycle and BLAST Premier data reviews, where lopsided head-to-head stats could prompt action—yet historical patterns show only 1-2 maps rotated yearly, tempering aggressive positioning.

question: Will $MARU reach $1B in Q1?
description:
This market will immediately resolve to “Yes” if any 1-minute candle for MARU (MARU/USDC) during Q1 2026 (from January 1 to 11:59 PM ET on March 31) has a final “High” price that implies a Fully Diluted Valuation (FDV) equal to or greater than the value specified in the title. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No.”

The FDV will be calculated by multiplying the MARU token price by the total token supply.

The resolution source for this market is EdgeX, specifically the MARU/USDC price feed available at:
https://pro.edgex.exchange/spot/MARU/USDC
with the chart set to “1m” (one-minute candles).

Only the EdgeX MARU/USDC price feed will be used. Prices from other exchanges, trading pairs, or data sources will not be considered.

question: Will Lebanese Forces win the most seats in the 2026 Lebanese parliamentary election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Lebanon in May 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Lebanese Parliament.

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Lebanese Parliament.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Lebanese Ministry of Interior and Municipalities (interior.gov.lb).

question: Will Popular Nasserist Organization win the most seats in the 2026 Lebanese parliamentary election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Lebanon in May 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Lebanese Parliament.

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Lebanese Parliament.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Lebanese Ministry of Interior and Municipalities (interior.gov.lb).

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.
