question: NATO dissolves before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) dissolves by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

NATO will be considered to be dissolved if any of the following conditions are met:

1) More than half of the NATO member states (as of market creation) withdraw from NATO.

2) An official treaty or agreement is adopted between all NATO member states to repeal or nullify the North Atlantic Treaty.
 
3) NATO otherwise ceases to exist as a legal entity.

NATO member states will be considered to have withdrawn once they officially initiate their withdrawal and/or provide an official notice of denunciation to NATO, regardless of whether the withdrawal is finalized after this market’s timeframe. A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from NATO and NATO member states; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: NATO dissolves before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) dissolves by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

NATO will be considered to be dissolved if any of the following conditions are met:

1) More than half of the NATO member states (as of market creation) withdraw from NATO.

2) An official treaty or agreement is adopted between all NATO member states to repeal or nullify the North Atlantic Treaty.
 
3) NATO otherwise ceases to exist as a legal entity.

NATO member states will be considered to have withdrawn once they officially initiate their withdrawal and/or provide an official notice of denunciation to NATO, regardless of whether the withdrawal is finalized after this market’s timeframe. A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from NATO and NATO member states; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: NATO's 75th anniversary summit in Washington last month solidified alliance unity, with 32 members pledging sustained support for Ukraine, defense spending increases toward 2% of GDP, and new pledges against Russian aggression, driving trader consensus toward a 91.5% implied probability that the alliance endures past 2027. Finland and Sweden's recent accessions have expanded NATO's footprint, while no official statements or votes signal dissolution risks. Even amid U.S. election rhetoric on burden-sharing, historical precedents like post-Cold War adaptations underscore resilience, with markets pricing in minimal fracture odds despite geopolitical tensions. Upcoming defense ministerial meetings in October could further reinforce stability.

question: EU/NATO country announces peacekeeping force in Ukraine by December 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any NATO or EU member country officially announces that they will be sending troops to Ukraine as part of a peacekeeping force by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying announcement must be part of a formal agreement between a NATO or EU member country and another country or international organization or otherwise indicative of a formalized policy.

Announcements which are statements of intent, contingent, or otherwise are not indicative of a formalized policy will not count

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from NATO, the EU, or member states of either entity, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: EU/NATO country announces peacekeeping force in Ukraine by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any NATO or EU member country officially announces that they will be sending troops to Ukraine as part of a peacekeeping force by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying announcement must be part of a formal agreement between a NATO or EU member country and another country or international organization or otherwise indicative of a formalized policy.

Announcements which are statements of intent, contingent, or otherwise are not indicative of a formalized policy will not count

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from NATO, the EU, or member states of either entity, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: No EU or NATO member has announced a peacekeeping force for Ukraine, anchoring trader consensus at low implied probabilities amid fears of Russian escalation. Western leaders, including France's Macron and Germany's Scholz, have reiterated support through arms and training but ruled out direct troop deployments, prioritizing NATO's no-boots-on-ground policy formalized at the July 2024 Washington summit. Recent Zelenskyy appeals for security guarantees have yielded no commitments, while Poland and Baltic states voice stronger rhetoric without formal pledges. The U.S. election on November 5 and potential Trump-mediated peace talks represent key catalysts that could shift dynamics, though historical caution in proxy conflicts tempers expectations.

question: US x Russia military clash by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Russia between January 6 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between U.S. and Russian military forces. Non-violent actions, such as airspace violations, firing of warning shots (such as the June, 2021 Black Sea Confrontations between Russian forces and HMS Defender), or cyberattacks will not qualify. 

Intentional physical collisions, including aerial interceptions and naval ramming without the direct use of weaponry, such as the 2023 Black Sea incident—where a Russian Su-27 damaged a U.S. MQ-9 Reaper drone's propeller, leading to its crash— will not qualify regardless of damage. 

Military contractors will qualify only if confirmed to be operating under the direct command or coordination of the respective state’s armed forces (e.g. the Battle of Khasham would not qualify).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: US x Russia military clash by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Russia between May 28 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between U.S. and Russian military forces. Non-violent actions, such as airspace violations, firing of warning shots (such as the June, 2021 Black Sea Confrontations between Russian forces and HMS Defender), or cyberattacks will not qualify. 

Intentional physical collisions, including aerial interceptions and naval ramming without the direct use of weaponry, such as the 2023 Black Sea incident—where a Russian Su-27 damaged a U.S. MQ-9 Reaper drone's propeller, leading to its crash— will not qualify regardless of damage. 

Military contractors will qualify only if confirmed to be operating under the direct command or coordination of the respective state’s armed forces (e.g. the Battle of Khasham would not qualify).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probabilities for a direct US-Russia military clash, anchored by nuclear deterrence and both sides' incentives to limit the Ukraine proxy war. Recent Ukrainian incursions into Russia's Kursk region using US-approved ATACMS missiles prompted Putin warnings but no retaliatory escalation beyond intensified airstrikes. US aid packages totaling $61 billion continue bolstering Kyiv's defenses, while NATO bolsters its eastern flank amid Russian Donbas advances. The November 5 US presidential election looms as a pivotal catalyst, potentially altering aid flows under a new administration. Winter stalemate and December NATO meetings could further shape risk assessments.

question: Any Brazil STF Justice removed by impeachment before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any justice of the Brazil Supreme Federal Court (STF) is permanently removed from the court as a result of impeachment or a trial for a crime of responsibility by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Justices leaving the court due to term limits, voluntary resignation, or any other reason not resulting from impeachment or a trial for a crime of responsibility will not count.

Impeachments, trials for crimes of responsibility, suspensions, or other procedural measures will not alone suffice to resolve this market if they do not result in the permanent removal of a justice from the Brazil Supreme Federal Court.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Brazil; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Any Brazil STF Justice removed by impeachment before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any justice of the Brazil Supreme Federal Court (STF) is permanently removed from the court as a result of impeachment or a trial for a crime of responsibility by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Justices leaving the court due to term limits, voluntary resignation, or any other reason not resulting from impeachment or a trial for a crime of responsibility will not count.

Impeachments, trials for crimes of responsibility, suspensions, or other procedural measures will not alone suffice to resolve this market if they do not result in the permanent removal of a justice from the Brazil Supreme Federal Court.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Brazil; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favoring "No" at 81.5% stems from the steep procedural hurdles and stalled momentum in Brazil's impeachment process for STF justices, which demands a two-thirds supermajority in both the Chamber of Deputies and Senate—a threshold unmet in modern history. Chamber President Arthur Lira has repeatedly archived dozens of requests targeting Justice Alexandre de Moraes amid Bolsonaro allies' pushes, including a recent October 2024 petition dismissed without debate. Centrist congressional majorities under Lula's influence show no appetite for advancing such votes, with no scheduled proceedings before 2027 despite ongoing political tensions over STF rulings on free speech and election integrity.

question: Will United Russia win fewer than 280 seats in the next Russian State Duma election?
description:
Legislative elections are scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the number of seats held by United Russia in the State Duma as a result of the next Russian legislative election.

If results are not known definitively by May 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a United Russia supermajority in the 2026 State Duma election, with 340–354 seats at 39% implied probability edging out 355+ at 30%, reflecting the party's systemic advantages amid suppressed opposition. Key drivers include President Putin's March 2024 landslide reelection (87% vote share), bolstering party loyalty, alongside administrative resources, state media dominance, and fragmented rivals following Alexei Navalny's death and ongoing crackdowns. Recent Levada polls show United Russia party support around 40%, tempered by war fatigue and sanctions, keeping outcomes tight. Separation could arise from Ukraine conflict escalations, economic data releases, or the September 2025 party congress announcements ahead of the September 2026 vote.

question: Will Elena Rybakina win a Calendar Grand Slam in 2026?
description:
This is a market to predict who will win a women's tennis Calendar Grand Slam in 2026.

This market resolves to the single woman player who wins the Women's Singles titles at all four Grand Slam tournaments in 2026. The relevant 2026 Grand Slams are the Women's Singles tournaments in the 2026 Australian Open, French Open, U.S. Open, and Wimbledon.

For the purpose of this market, a tournament victory is valid regardless of whether the final was won via a walkover, a mid-match retirement, or a standard completed match.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to achieve a Calendar Grand Slam in 2026 (e.g. Sabalenka does not win the 2026 Australian Open) this market will resolve immediately to "No". 

If at any point it becomes impossible for any listed player to achieve a Calendar Grand Slam in 2026 (e.g. Sabalenka wins the 2026 Australian Open but loses in the first round of 2026 French Open) this market will resolve immediately to "None". 

Otherwise, this market will resolve when the results of the final 2026 Grand Slam are official.

If any of the 2026 Grand Slam Women' Singles Tournaments are cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “None”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the organizers of the Grand Slam tournaments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The extreme rarity of a women's calendar Grand Slam—last achieved by Steffi Graf in 1988—drives trader consensus to 98.2% for none in 2026, reflecting the WTA Tour's depth, surface specialists like Iga Swiatek on clay and Aryna Sabalenka on hard courts, and the physical toll of four majors amid injuries and fatigue. Elena Rybakina's slim 1.1% share stems from her powerful all-court game, Wimbledon title, and youth at 27, positioning her as the top longshot amid recent parity with four different 2024 major winners. Realistic challenges require unprecedented health, surface mastery across Australian Open, Roland Garros, Wimbledon, and US Open, plus avoiding upsets in a hyper-competitive field.

question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,000 (HIGH) in June?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for June 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

events.title: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of June?
events.description: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of June 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices the S&P 500 closing above 5,500 by June 30th at around 45%, reflecting a tight race amid recent record highs near 5,460 driven by cooler-than-expected May CPI inflation at 3.3% year-over-year, fueling hopes for Fed rate cuts. Strong tech earnings from Nvidia and others propelled a 3% weekly gain, but persistent high bond yields and geopolitical tensions cap upside. Key upcoming catalysts include June 28 PCE inflation data—the Fed's preferred gauge—and flash PMIs on June 24, which could sway sentiment; a softer print might push toward 5,500+, while hotter figures risk pullback to 5,400 range. Uncertainty lingers with just over a week left.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices the S&P 500 closing above 5,500 by June 30th at around 45%, reflecting a tight race amid recent record highs near 5,460 driven by cooler-than-expected May CPI inflation at 3.3% year-over-year, fueling hopes for Fed rate cuts. Strong tech earnings from Nvidia and others propelled a 3% weekly gain, but persistent high bond yields and geopolitical tensions cap upside. Key upcoming catalysts include June 28 PCE inflation data—the Fed's preferred gauge—and flash PMIs on June 24, which could sway sentiment; a softer print might push toward 5,500+, while hotter figures risk pullback to 5,400 range. Uncertainty lingers with just over a week left.

question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $9,300 (HIGH) in December?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for December 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

events.title: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of December?
events.description: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of December 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 82% implied probability for the S&P 500 closing above 6,000 by December 31, reflecting bullish sentiment fueled by the post-election rally that pushed the index to a record 6,025 intraday on November 14 amid expectations of pro-growth policies under President-elect Trump, including tax cuts and deregulation. The Federal Reserve's 25-basis-point rate cut on November 7, with dot-plot signaling two more in 2025, has eased recession fears and supported risk assets. Key catalysts ahead include the December 18 FOMC meeting, November CPI/PPI data releases, and Q3 earnings wrap-up, though tariff risks and fiscal deficit concerns cap upside; historical December seasonality favors gains averaging 1.3% since 1950.

question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) close at <$6,000 in December?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for S&P 500 (SPX) on the final trading day of December 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the S&P 500 (SPX) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/history, published under "Historical Prices."

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the S&P 500's 2026 year-end close tilts bearish, with a 29% implied probability for sub-$6,000 reflecting concerns over stretched valuations and slowing earnings growth amid persistent inflation risks. Currently hovering near 5,900, the index would require only modest 1-2% annualized gains to hit $6,000, yet competitive odds across $6,000-$7,500 bins (totaling 63%) highlight divided views on Fed rate cuts sustaining momentum versus recession fears. Key differentiators include projected 2026 EPS growth of 10-12% from AI-driven tech gains, balanced against potential multiple compression if yields rise; watch Q4 2025 earnings and 2026 GDP releases for shifts in trader consensus.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the S&P 500 (SPX) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/history, published under "Historical Prices."

question: Will Nasdaq 100 (NDX) hit $45,000 (HIGH) in December?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for December 2026, any 1-minute candle for Nasdaq 100 (NDX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for Nasdaq 100 (NDX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5ENDX/.

events.title: What will Nasdaq 100 (NDX) hit by end of December?
events.description: What will Nasdaq 100 (NDX) hit by end of December 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies a modest year-end rally for the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), with odds favoring levels above 20,500 by December 31, fueled by post-election optimism and expectations of a Federal Reserve rate cut at the December 18 FOMC meeting. Currently hovering near 20,200 after a 25% YTD gain driven by AI-fueled Big Tech strength, NDX faces tailwinds from easing monetary policy—traders price in 90% odds of a 25bps cut amid cooling inflation (October CPI at 2.6% YoY)—but risks persist from potential fiscal stimulus inflating yields or geopolitical tensions. Watch December 11 CPI and December 6 NFP for volatility, as sustained 4-5% 10-year Treasury yields could cap upside.
